other_observers: Cole Wild, Solomon Miller, Georgia Doyle; Three of us spotted this bird simultaneously and independently. It is identical to the bird that has been seen regularly in Windsor since June 1 and was seen by Cole Wild and Sol Miller near this Larimer County location on June 13
specific_location: West end of Fossil Creek Reservoir
description: Huge bird, shape of a pelican, but smaller then accompanying American White Pelican. Pale grayish brown body, with chestnut brown, long neck except for dark yellow skin on throat bordered by bright white. The white extended up the neck to the head and forecrown. Hind crown and nape was chestnut-brown like hindneck. Huge horn-colored bill.  In flight, flapped constantly but slowly, maintaining an altitude of about 5 feet over the lake water.Behaviors: Loafing on a mud-spit then flew low over lakeCall: nonePlumage: subadult
photographer: Photographer: Nick Komar, Nick Komar, Nick Komar, Nick Komar, Nick Komar
